title: pittosporum wightii
intro: Pittosporum wightii, also known as Anoli or Indian cherry, is a deciduous shrub native to the Western Ghats region of India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. It is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing protection against various diseases. Pittosporum wightii can be prepared into herbal preparations like extracts, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
